body:
This PR restructures the AGENTS.md file, moving much of its content into specific `.claude/rules` or `.agents` documents.

The idea is that agent performance is strongly affected by how targeted the instructions are that it receives. Therefore, a too-long `AGENTS.md` can degrade performance, because it receives a lot of information that is irrelevant to the specific task it needs to solve in that moment.

Some of this is discussed on this post:

https://www.humanlayer.dev/blog/writing-a-good-claude-md

And was also mentioned by @jkrasting on a similar PR to MOM6: https://github.com/mom-ocean/MOM6/pull/1691

I also look the liberty of adding some `skills`. Note, I am trying to include both claude-specific information (in `.claude`) as well as tool-agnostic information (in `.agents`) but it may not be ideal to have to duplicate information. Still, it is not too difficult to keep this all up to date.

I generated a lot of this automatically using our original `AGENTS.md` as a starting point. I also took some inspiration from a technique I used for MOM6 and asked Claude to analyze all PRs from the last two years to find additional best practices and tips.
title: Restructure AGENTS.md into multi-file agent configuration

body: Added two new papers to the list of works using Oceananigans.
title: Update papers section in index.md

body:
## Summary

Cross-referenced ~103 Semantic Scholar citations of the JOSS paper (Ramadhan et al. 2020), Google Scholar citations of the overview preprint (Wagner et al. 2025), and citations of the model development papers against the current reference list, then verified each candidate by reading the paper to confirm Oceananigans usage.

These 9 papers are confirmed to use Oceananigans but were missing from the list:

- [De Abreu & Timmermans (2026)](https://doi.org/10.1175/JPO-D-25-0165.1) — "Mixed-layer deepening and internal wave generation under sea ice in free drift" (*JPO*)
- [Allende, Couston, Thalabard & Favier (2026)](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2601.18674) — "Melting dynamics and mixing layer growth near the ice-ocean interface" (*arXiv*)
- [Middleton et al. (2025)](https://doi.org/10.1126/sciadv.adu3221) — "Observations of a splitting ocean cyclone resulting in subduction of surface waters" (*Science Advances*)
- [Pružina, Zhou, Middleton & Taylor (2025)](https://doi.org/10.1017/jfm.2025.258) — "Layer formation in double-diffusive convection diagnosed in sorted buoyancy coordinates" (*JFM*)
- [Pružina (2025)](https://doi.org/10.1017/jfm.2025.141) — "A one-dimensional model of staircase formation in diffusive convection" (*JFM*)
- [Lee, Hutchings, Horvat, Tavri & Pearson (2025)](https://doi.org/10.5194/egusphere-2025-4239) — "Impact of Surface Waves on Mixing and Circulation in a Summertime Lead" (*EGUsphere preprint*)
- [Fromme, Harder, Allen-Blanchette & Peitz (2025)](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2505.13569) — "Surrogate Modeling of 3D Rayleigh-Bénard Convection with Equivariant Autoencoders" (*arXiv*)
- [Zhang, Kang & Marshall (2024)](https://doi.org/10.1126/sciadv.adn6857) — "Ocean weather systems on icy moons, with application to Enceladus" (*Science Advances*)
- [Bire et al. (2023)](https://doi.org/10.1029/2023JE007740) — "Divergent behavior of hydrothermal plumes in fresh versus salty icy ocean worlds" (*JGR Planets*)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add 9 missing papers to the reference list
Add new reference to Zheng et al. (2025) in index

body:
## Summary

- Adds a new entry to Common Pitfalls warning agents not to modify `[deps]` or `[weakdeps]` in the root `Project.toml` unless absolutely required
- Dependency changes affect CI, load time, and downstream compatibility — agents should not make them casually

## Test plan

- [ ] No functional code changes — documentation only

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Add dependency guardrail to Common Pitfalls

body:
## Summary

- Fixes a bug where `ContinuousForcing` with `field_dependencies` (including `Relaxation`) silently read the wrong field in `HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel`
- The `model_fields` used during forcing materialization was built from `hydrostatic_prognostic_fields` `(u, v, T, S, η)` which excludes `w`, while the `model_fields` in tendency kernels uses `hydrostatic_fields` `(u, v, w, T, S, η)` which includes `w`
- This shifted all tracer indices by 1 — e.g. a forcing with `field_dependencies=:T` would read `w` instead of `T` at runtime

## Fix

One-line change: use `hydrostatic_fields` (which includes `w`) instead of `prognostic_fields` when building `model_fields` for forcing materialization, matching what the tendency kernels use.

Closes #5369

## Test plan

- [x] Reproduced the MWE from #5369: continuous and discrete forms now produce identical results
- [x] Verified `field_dependencies_indices` now maps to the correct field at tendency computation time
- [ ] Existing test suite

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: Fix ContinuousForcing field index mismatch in HydrostaticFreeSurfaceModel
(0.105.3) Fix interpolation of terms in chain horizontal derivatives for MutableVerticalDiscretization
